summary: Static testability-surface analyzer that surfaces welded boundary calls (clock, subprocess, network, file I/O, randomness) with no seam to inject a failure — a deterministic, zero-LLM testability backlog, not a bug list; intended as an advisory input to the `quality` skill.

notes: Preferred path is the upstream release installer (cargo-dist, the nose model), which downloads a prebuilt `pry` binary onto PATH.
Homebrew is not yet available — shell installer only as of v0.1.0.
Use `PRY_BIN=/abs/path/to/pry/target/release/pry` only as a maintainer-local override when testing a sibling checkout.
Rerun the release installer to update; it fetches the latest published binary.

detect.failure_hint: Install `pry` via the release installer (see lifecycle.install) or set `PRY_BIN`, before using the quality testability backlog.

healthcheck.failure_hint: The `pry` binary exists but its `map` surface is unavailable. Reinstall or upgrade from the latest release.

when_missing: Quality keeps its deterministic gates authoritative and omits the welded-at-demand testability backlog.
Do not claim a testability or injectability review succeeded without a healthy `pry` binary.
when_version_mismatch: Treat changes in welded/seamed classification, demand-subset ranking, or JSON output shape as advisory drift before promoting findings into gates.

notes: Prefer the upstream pry support skill (the intelligence layer that runs the binary, ranks the welded-at-demand backlog, and labels each finding GENUINE/FALSE-WELD/COSMETIC/AMBIGUOUS) instead of reimplementing that labeling in charness.

The installed Charness plugin materializes this skill under `support/pry/` during update or support sync; charness still owns the discover/install/doctor manifest plus the exit-0 advisory inventory wrapper so the testability backlog never fails standing quality.

host_notes: `pry` is an advisory input to Charness `quality`: it proposes a testability backlog (welded boundary calls with no injection seam), not a bug list, and must never fail standing quality. This manifest makes `pry` discoverable and doctor-checkable; `quality` auto-runs it as the advisory `inventory-testability-surface` phase in `scripts/run-quality.sh` (via `skills/public/quality/scripts/inventory_testability_surface.py`, wrapping `pry map` and degrading when absent). The testability backlog contract is in `skills/public/quality/references/testability-and-selection.md`; the synced `support/pry` skill owns per-finding labeling.

pry analyzes TypeScript/JavaScript today — the validated surface (see pry `docs/precision-gate.md`). A Python frontend is out of scope: the author's Python is welded glue with no discrimination (pry `docs/kill-gate.md`).
Run `pry map <path>` for the full finding map, or `pry map <path> --summary-only` for the coverage summary; the `demand=true` substitution-demand subset is the ranked backlog.
